evals.prompt:
Tailor my resume for this job. My resume is at evals/resume-tailor/fixtures/sample_resume.tex.

Here's the posting:

Backend Engineer — Distributed Systems @ Vantyr
Vantyr is building a real-time payments platform. We're hiring a Backend Engineer to design and operate low-latency, high-throughput services.

What you'll do:
- Design and build RESTful and gRPC microservices in Go
- Run services on Kubernetes; own deployment, scaling, and on-call
- Model financial data in PostgreSQL and optimize query performance
- Build CI/CD pipelines and improve observability (metrics, tracing, logging)
- Participate in design reviews and mentor junior engineers

Requirements:
- 3+ years building backend services in production
- Strong with at least one of Go, Java, or Python
- Experience with containers (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes)
- Solid SQL and relational database experience (PostgreSQL preferred)
- Familiarity with CI/CD and cloud platforms (AWS)

Nice to have: gRPC, Redis, distributed systems, event-driven architecture.
evals.expected_output: A kebab-case folder (e.g. vantyr-backend-engineer) in the current directory containing the tailored sample_resume.tex, a compiled sample_resume.pdf, and CHANGES.md. The resume should surface backend/Python/PostgreSQL/Docker/AWS/CI-CD/Redis experience prominently using the JD's exact terminology (RESTful, microservices, CI/CD pipelines, observability). Go and Kubernetes are absent-but-adjacent (Docker present, no K8s; Python present, no Go) — these should be added as defensible stretches and flagged in CHANGES.md's 'Review before you submit' section. The .tex must still compile.

assertions.name: kebab-case-folder
assertions.description: An output folder is created in the current directory whose name is derived from the company/role in kebab-case (e.g. vantyr-backend-engineer), not the literal fixture name.

assertions.name: tex-and-pdf-present
assertions.description: The output folder contains both a .tex file and a compiled .pdf of the resume.

assertions.name: pdf-compiles
assertions.description: The output .tex compiles to a valid non-empty PDF (the build script ran successfully).

assertions.name: exact-keyword-mirroring
assertions.description: The tailored resume uses the JD's exact terminology where the original experience supports it — e.g. 'microservices', 'CI/CD', 'observability', 'PostgreSQL' (not 'Postgres') appear in the text.

assertions.name: stretches-flagged
assertions.description: CHANGES.md exists and its 'Review before you submit' section explicitly flags any technologies that were stretched beyond the original (e.g. Go and/or Kubernetes), rather than silently inserting them.

assertions.name: no-fabricated-facts
assertions.description: No fabricated employers, job titles, dates, or degrees were introduced; work history matches the original resume's companies and timeline.

assertions.name: template-preserved
assertions.description: The document class, packages, and custom macros from the original template are preserved; only content within the structure changed.

evals.prompt:
Can you optimize evals/resume-tailor/fixtures/sample_resume.tex for this role? Want it to do well in their ATS.

Frontend Engineer (React/TypeScript) — Loomly
We build collaborative design tools. Looking for a Frontend Engineer to own key parts of our web app.

Responsibilities:
- Build and maintain features in React with TypeScript
- Work closely with designers to ship polished, accessible UI
- Consume and help design REST APIs
- Write unit and integration tests; contribute to our CI pipeline

Requirements:
- 2+ years building production frontend with React
- Proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript and modern CSS
- Experience integrating REST APIs
- Familiarity with testing and CI/CD

Nice to have: TypeScript, Next.js, design systems, web performance.
evals.expected_output: A kebab-case folder (e.g. loomly-frontend-engineer) with tailored .tex, compiled .pdf, and CHANGES.md. The skill should pull the candidate's React/JavaScript/REST-integration experience (currently buried in the older 'Junior Developer' role and skills) forward and prominent, since the most recent role is backend-leaning. The summary/title should be reframed toward frontend. TypeScript is absent-but-adjacent to JavaScript and should be a flagged stretch. Should not over-claim deep frontend seniority given the actual experience.

assertions.name: kebab-case-folder
assertions.description: An output folder named in kebab-case from the company/role (e.g. loomly-frontend-engineer) is created in the current directory.

assertions.name: tex-and-pdf-present
assertions.description: The folder contains both the tailored .tex and a compiled .pdf.

assertions.name: frontend-surfaced
assertions.description: React / JavaScript / REST-integration experience is made prominent (e.g. reordered earlier, expanded, or pulled into a summary), rather than left buried under the backend role.

assertions.name: title-reframed
assertions.description: A summary or headline reframes the candidate toward the frontend role's title/keywords without falsifying the actual job titles in the work history.

assertions.name: typescript-flagged
assertions.description: If TypeScript (absent from the original) is added, CHANGES.md flags it as a stretch in the 'Review before you submit' section.

assertions.name: changes-report-coverage
assertions.description: CHANGES.md includes an ATS keyword coverage table mapping JD keywords to where they appear in the tailored resume.

evals.prompt:
I need evals/resume-tailor/fixtures/iconheader_resume.tex tailored for the job below — and I've heard my resume formatting might be hurting me with automated screeners, so fix that too while you're at it.

Senior Full-Stack Engineer @ Cedarpoint
We're a Series B company building B2B SaaS. Seeking a Senior Full-Stack Engineer to lead features across our React/TypeScript frontend and Node.js/GraphQL backend.

What we need:
- 5+ years full-stack web development
- Expert in React and TypeScript
- Strong Node.js and GraphQL API design
- Experience leading projects and mentoring engineers
- CI/CD, AWS, and PostgreSQL experience
- Strong communication; comfortable running design reviews
evals.expected_output: A kebab-case folder (e.g. cedarpoint-senior-full-stack-engineer) with tailored .tex, compiled .pdf, and CHANGES.md. This candidate is a strong match, so tailoring is mostly reframing/keyword-mirroring. Critically, the skill should fix the ATS formatting pitfalls in this template: contact info currently lives in the fancyhdr page header (often unreadable by ATS) and should be moved into the document body; nonstandard headings 'What I Do', 'Where I've Worked', 'Stack', 'School' should be normalized to standard ATS-recognizable headings (Summary, Experience, Skills, Education). The .tex must still compile.

assertions.name: kebab-case-folder
assertions.description: A kebab-case output folder from the company/role (e.g. cedarpoint-senior-full-stack-engineer) is created in the current directory.

assertions.name: tex-and-pdf-present
assertions.description: The folder contains both the tailored .tex and a compiled .pdf.

assertions.name: contact-moved-to-body
assertions.description: Contact info (name, email, phone, links) is moved out of the fancyhdr header into the document body so an ATS can read it.

assertions.name: headings-normalized
assertions.description: Nonstandard section headings are normalized to standard ATS-recognizable ones (e.g. 'What I Do'->Summary, 'Where I've Worked'->Experience, 'Stack'->Skills, 'School'->Education).

assertions.name: keyword-mirroring
assertions.description: JD keywords the candidate genuinely has (React, TypeScript, Node.js, GraphQL, CI/CD, AWS, PostgreSQL, mentoring, design reviews) are present using the JD's exact terms.

assertions.name: pdf-compiles
assertions.description: The restructured .tex still compiles to a valid PDF despite the formatting changes.

assertions.name: changes-documented
assertions.description: CHANGES.md documents the formatting fixes (header->body, heading normalization) so the user understands what changed and why.
